content.text: This poem is a humorous piece about a sage who lived in the days of yore (long ago). He had a handsome pigtail, but he was completely dissatisfied and upset because it hung behind him. He pondered over this 'mystery' and wanted to change its position so that it would hang at his face. To achieve this, he spun all day, twisted, twirled, tacked, turned right and left, up and down, and in and out. Despite his non-stop and desperate efforts (which never slacked), he failed completely. The pigtail faithfully remained hanging behind his back. The poem uses comedy and irony to show the absurdity of trying to change something that is natural and unchangeable.

The poem is written by William Makepeace Thackeray. He was one of the great novelists of the English Victorian Age. His most famous and best-known novel in English literature is 'Vanity Fair'. Thackeray's writing style is characterized by being colorful and lively, using a simple vocabulary and clearly-structured sentences. He is known for his honest view of life, which gives him an important place in the history of realistic literature.

1. sage - wise man
2. yore - long ago
3. pigtail - a plaited lock of hair worn singly at the back
4. mused - thought over
5. curious - eager to learn more
6. mystery - puzzle
7. stout - thick in structure

1. Irony: Irony is a figure of speech in which words are used in such a way that their intended meaning is different from the actual meaning. It is the difference between appearance and reality. The poem is ironic because a 'sage' is supposed to be wise, but he does a very foolish act of trying to change the natural position of his pigtail.
2. Alliteration: 'He mused upon this curious case' (The consonant sound 'c' is repeated in curious and case).
3. Rhyme Scheme: In the stanza ending with slack, tack, back, him - the rhyme scheme is 'a a a b'.
4. Rhyming Words: slack - tack - back.

Do not miss the qualities of Thackeray: 'colorful, lively style', 'simple vocabulary', 'clearly-structured sentences', and 'honest view of life'. The genre is 'realistic literature' and the era is 'Victorian Age'. The words twist, twirl, and tack represent the physical actions taken by the sage to achieve an impossible task.

**கேள்வி:** 1. What made the sage upset?
**விடை:** The sage was upset because his handsome pigtail hung behind him and he wanted it to hang at his face.

**கேள்வி:** 2. Why did the sage spin all day?
**விடை:** He spun all day in an attempt to change the position of his pigtail from his back to his face.

**கேள்வி:** 3. What solution did he arrive at for the mystery that he found?
**விடை:** He arrived at the solution that if he turned him round, the pigtail would change its place.

**கேள்வி:** 4. Was he finally successful in changing his pigtail’s position? Support your answer with a line from the poem.
**விடை:** No, he was not successful. The line 'Alas! Still faithful to his back / The pigtail hangs behind him' supports this.

**கேள்வி:** 5. Did something dreadful happen? How would you describe the events in the poem– comedy or tragedy?
**விடை:** No, nothing dreadful happened. Even though the title is 'A Tragic Story', the events describe a pure comedy because of the sage's foolish actions.

**கேள்வி:** 6. 'But wondered much and sorrowed more / Because it hung behind him.' - a) What was he wondering about? b) What does the word 'it' refer to here?
**விடை:** a) He was wondering about the position of his pigtail. b) 'It' refers to the pigtail.

**கேள்வி:** 7. 'And though his efforts never slack / And though he twist, and twirl, and tack / Alas! Still faithful to his back / The pigtail hangs behind him.' - a) Pick out the rhyming words and give the rhyme scheme. b) Did he quit his trying? How can you say?
**விடை:** a) Rhyming words: slack - tack - back. Rhyme scheme: a a a b. b) No, he didn't quit trying. The line 'And though his efforts never slack' proves this.

**கேள்வி:** 8. 'He mused upon this curious case' - What is the figure of speech used in this line?
**விடை:** Alliteration (curious, case).

**கேள்வி:** 9. Can this poem be called an ironic poem? Justify your answer.
**விடை:** Yes, it is ironic. A sage is considered to be a highly wise and intelligent person, but here he wastes his time doing a foolish and impossible task of making his pigtail hang in front of his face.

**கேள்வி:** 10. Missing words Summary fill-up:
**விடை:** Once upon a time there lived a SAGE. He had a handsome PIGTAIL. He was worried and pondered over his pigtail's MYSTERY. He wanted to CHANGE its place. He wanted it hanging at his FACE. He didn’t like it hanging there BEHIND him. So he turned right and left and ROUND about, up and DOWN, and in and OUT, but it still HUNG behind him. However he tried, his efforts were in VAIN. But he didn’t SLACK in his efforts. Nevertheless his pigtail hung behind him.

William Makepeace Thackeray || William Makepeace Thackeray
John Keats || John Keats
Robert Frost || Robert Frost

options: A type of herb || A type of herb

questions.q: Read the lines: 'But wondered much and sorrowed more / Because it hung behind him.' What does the word 'it' refer to? || ஆனால் அதிகம் ஆச்சரியப்பட்டார், மேலும் வருத்தப்பட்டார் / ஏனென்றால் அது அவருக்குப் பின்னால் தொங்கியது என்ற வரிகளைப் படிக்கவும். அது (it) என்ற சொல் எதைக் குறிக்கிறது?

questions.q: What figure of speech is defined as 'a difference between appearance and reality' or when intended meaning differs from actual meaning? || தோற்றத்திற்கும் உண்மைக்கும் இடையிலான வேறுபாடு என்று எந்த அணி வரையறுக்கப்படுகிறது?

Irony || Irony

questions.ex: The irony lies in the fact that a 'sage' (appearance of wisdom) wastes his time trying to do something logically impossible (reality of foolishness). || The irony lies in the fact that a 'sage' (appearance of wisdom) wastes his time trying to do something logically impossible (reality of foolishness).

questions.q: Find the figure of speech used in the line: 'He mused upon this curious case'. || முனிவர் ஒரு விசித்திரமான வழக்கைப் பற்றி mused செய்தார். mused என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?

Simile || உவமை அணி
Hyperbole || Hyperbole
Onomatopoeia || Onomatopoeia

questions.q: Identify the rhyme scheme for the given stanza: 'And though his efforts never slack / And though he twist, and twirl, and tack, / Alas! Still faithful to his back / The pigtail hangs behind him.' || pigtail (பின்னல்) என்றால் என்ன?
options: a b a b || a b a b
a a a b || a a a b
a b b a || a b b a
a a b b || a a b b

questions.q: Which of the following sets of words from the poem are rhyming words? || கவிதையில் உள்ள பின்வரும் சொற்களின் தொகுப்புகளில் எவை எதுகை-மோனைச் சொற்கள்?

twist - twirl - tack || twist - twirl - tack
mused - curious - case || mused - curious - case
yore - back - him || yore - back - him
questions.ex: Slack, tack, and back all end with the identical '-ack' sound, making them rhyming words. || Slack, tack, and back all end with the identical '-ack' sound, making them rhyming words.

He only tried once || He only tried once
He asked someone else to do it || He asked someone else to do it

questions.q: Which of the following actions did the sage NOT perform while trying to change the pigtail's position? || கவிதையின் சூழலில் sage (முனிவர்) என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: Twist || Twist
Twirl || Twirl
Tack || Tack

questions.q:
Match the following glossary words with their meanings:
1. Yore - i. thick in structure
2. Stout - ii. puzzle
3. Mystery - iii. long ago
4. Mused - iv. thought over || உரையில் உள்ள எந்தச் சொல் அமைப்பில் தடிமனான என்று பொருள்படும்?

1-i, 2-ii, 3-iii, 4-iv || 1-i, 2-ii, 3-iii, 4-iv
1-iv, 2-iii, 3-ii, 4-i || 1-iv, 2-iii, 3-ii, 4-i
1-ii, 2-i, 3-iv, 4-iii || 1-ii, 2-i, 3-iv, 4-iii
questions.ex: Yore = long ago; Stout = thick in structure; Mystery = puzzle; Mused = thought over. || Yore = long ago; Stout = thick in structure; Mystery = puzzle; Mused = thought over.

questions.q: Analogy: Vanity Fair : William Makepeace Thackeray :: Realistic literature : ? || ஒப்புமை: வேனிட்டி ஃபேர் : வில்லியம் மேக்பீஸ் தாக்கரே :: யதார்த்தமான இலக்கியம் : ?
options: William Wordsworth || William Wordsworth
John Keats || John Keats
William Makepeace Thackeray || William Makepeace Thackeray
Robert Frost || Robert Frost

questions.q: Analogy based on meaning: Yore : Present :: Stout : ? || பொருளின் அடிப்படையிலான ஒப்புமை: யோர் : நிகழ்காலம் :: ஸ்டவுட் : ?
options: Thick || Thick
Thin || Thin
Fat || Fat
Strong || Strong
questions.ex: Yore (past/long ago) and Present are antonyms. The antonym of Stout (thick in structure) is Thin. || Yore (past/long ago) and Present are antonyms. The antonym of Stout (thick in structure) is Thin.

questions.q:
Find the correct statement from the following:
Statement A: The sage was a young boy with no hair.
Statement B: The sage wanted his pigtail to hang behind his back. || கவிதையின் சூழலில் sage (முனிவர்) என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: Only Statement A is correct || Only Statement A is correct
Only Statement B is correct || Only Statement B is correct
Both Statements are correct || Both Statements are correct
Neither Statement A nor B is correct || Neither Statement A nor B is correct
questions.ex: Statement A is wrong because a sage implies an older, wise man, and he definitely had hair (a pigtail). Statement B is wrong because he wanted the pigtail to hang at his face, not behind his back. || Statement A is wrong because a sage implies an older, wise man, and he definitely had hair (a pigtail). Statement B is wrong because he wanted the pigtail to hang at his face, not behind his back.

questions.q:
Assertion (A): The poem 'A Tragic Story' is an excellent example of situational irony.
Reason (R): A supposedly wise sage acts in a foolish manner trying to physically turn around to catch his pigtail that is attached to the back of his own head. || கவிதையின் சூழலில் sage (முனிவர்) என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A || Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A || Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false || A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true || A is false but R is true
questions.ex: Irony occurs when the reality is completely different from the appearance. The wise man doing an absolutely foolish task validates the assertion. || Irony occurs when the reality is completely different from the appearance. The wise man doing an absolutely foolish task validates the assertion.

questions.q: Fill in the blanks based on the poem's summary: Once upon a time there lived a _________. He had a handsome ________. || கோடிட்ட இடங்களை நிரப்புக: ஒரு காலத்தில் ஒரு _________ வாழ்ந்து வந்தார். அவருக்கு ஒரு அழகான ________ இருந்தது.
options: king, crown || king, crown
sage, pigtail || sage, pigtail
boy, dog || boy, dog
tailor, needle || tailor, needle
questions.ex: The summary clearly states: 'Once upon a time there lived a sage. He had a handsome pigtail.' || The summary clearly states: 'Once upon a time there lived a sage. He had a handsome pigtail.'

questions.q: Choose the correct sequence of actions the sage performed as per the summary fill-in-the-blanks. || கவிதையின் சூழலில் sage (முனிவர்) என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: up and down -> in and out -> right and left || up and down -> in and out -> right and left
right and left -> round about -> up and down -> in and out || right and left -> round about -> up and down -> in and out
in and out -> right and left -> round about || in and out -> right and left -> round about
round about -> in and out -> up and down || round about -> in and out -> up and down
questions.ex: The book back text reads: 'So he turned right and left and round about, up and down, and in and out...' || The book back text reads: 'So he turned right and left and round about, up and down, and in and out...'

questions.q: What does the word 'faithful' imply in the line 'Still faithful to his back'? || ஆனால் அதிகம் ஆச்சரியப்பட்டார், மேலும் வருத்தப்பட்டார் / ஏனென்றால் அது அவருக்குப் பின்னால் தொங்கியது என்ற வரிகளைப் படிக்கவும். அது (it) என்ற சொல் எதைக் குறிக்கிறது?
options: The pigtail was a pet dog || The pigtail was a pet dog
The pigtail naturally and constantly remained in its anatomical place || The pigtail naturally and constantly remained in its anatomical place
The sage loved his back || The sage loved his back
The back was waiting for the pigtail || The back was waiting for the pigtail
questions.ex: Personification is used humorously. The pigtail is 'faithful' because it never leaves its natural spot at the back of his head. || Personification is used humorously. The pigtail is 'faithful' because it never leaves its natural spot at the back of his head.

questions.q: To pantomime the poem means to: || கவிதையை பேண்டோமைம் செய்வது என்றால்:
options: Sing the poem loudly || Sing the poem loudly
Communicate by means of gesture and facial expression while reading || Communicate by means of gesture and facial expression while reading
Translate the poem into another language || Translate the poem into another language
Write an essay about the poem || Write an essay about the poem
questions.ex: The role-play instruction clearly defines pantomime as 'communication by means of gesture and facial expression'. || The role-play instruction clearly defines pantomime as 'communication by means of gesture and facial expression'.

questions.q: Why are William Makepeace Thackeray's sentences described as 'clearly-structured'? || தாக்கரேயின் வாக்கியங்கள் ஏன் தெளிவாக-கட்டமைக்கப்பட்டவை என்று விவரிக்கப்படுகின்றன?
options: Because they are very short || Because they are very short
Because he used foreign words || Because he used foreign words
Because they make the reading lively and easy to understand with simple vocabulary || Because they make the reading lively and easy to understand with simple vocabulary
Because they contain many spelling mistakes || Because they contain many spelling mistakes
questions.ex: The text mentions that his colorful, lively style combined with simple vocabulary and clearly-structured sentences makes him a great realistic writer. || The text mentions that his colorful, lively style combined with simple vocabulary and clearly-structured sentences makes him a great realistic writer.

questions.q: Which word from the text acts as a synonym for 'puzzle'? || Alas என்ற சொல் எந்த உணர்ச்சியைக் குறிக்கிறது?
options: Yore || Yore
Mystery || Mystery
Stout || Stout
Slack || Slack
questions.ex: The glossary explicitly maps 'mystery' to 'puzzle'. || The glossary explicitly maps 'mystery' to 'puzzle'.

questions.q: The sage wanted his pigtail hanging at his ________. || கவிதையின் சூழலில் sage (முனிவர்) என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: Back || Back
Shoulder || Shoulder
Face || Face
Neck || Neck
questions.ex: From the summary fill-in exercise: 'He wanted it hanging at his face.' || From the summary fill-in exercise: 'He wanted it hanging at his face.'

questions.q: However he tried, his efforts were in ________. || அவர் எவ்வளவு முயன்றாலும், அவரது முயற்சிகள் ________ இல் முடிந்தன.
options: Vain || Vain
Gain || Gain
Joy || Joy
Success || Success
questions.ex: From the summary fill-in exercise: 'However he tried, his efforts were in vain.' (vain means useless or unsuccessful). || From the summary fill-in exercise: 'However he tried, his efforts were in vain.' (vain means useless or unsuccessful).

questions.q: If you are 'eager to learn more' about something, you are said to be ________. || நீங்கள் எதையாவது பற்றி மேலும் அறிய ஆர்வமாக இருந்தால், நீங்கள் ________ என்று கூறப்படுவீர்கள்.
options: Mused || Mused
Stout || Stout
Curious || Curious
Sage || Sage
questions.ex: The glossary definition of curious is 'eager to learn more'. || The glossary definition of curious is 'eager to learn more'.

questions.q: What phrase is used to represent the physical struggle of the sage? || கவிதையின் சூழலில் sage (முனிவர்) என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: wondered much and sorrowed more || wondered much and sorrowed more
twist, and twirl, and tack || twist, and twirl, and tack
mused upon this curious case || mused upon this curious case
days of yore || days of yore
questions.ex: These three verbs represent the physical spinning, turning, and jumping actions the sage took. || These three verbs represent the physical spinning, turning, and jumping actions the sage took.

questions.q: The title 'A Tragic Story' is in itself an example of which literary device? || A Tragic Story என்ற தலைப்பு எந்த இலக்கிய சாதனத்திற்கு ஒரு எடுத்துக்காட்டு?
options: Simile || உவமை அணி
Irony || Irony
Personification || Personification
Metaphor || Metaphor
questions.ex: The title suggests a tragedy (something very sad or fatal), but the actual poem is purely a comedy about a foolish man. Hence, the title itself is ironic. || The title suggests a tragedy (something very sad or fatal), but the actual poem is purely a comedy about a foolish man. Hence, the title itself is ironic.

questions.q: Which of the following defines 'pigtail' correctly as per the text? || pigtail (பின்னல்) என்றால் என்ன?
options: Two plaited locks of hair on the sides || Two plaited locks of hair on the sides
A plaited lock of hair worn singly at the back || A plaited lock of hair worn singly at the back
A loosely hanging open hair || A loosely hanging open hair
A bald patch on the head || A bald patch on the head
questions.ex: The glossary specifies it is a 'plaited lock of hair worn singly at the back'. || The glossary specifies it is a 'plaited lock of hair worn singly at the back'.

questions.q:
Assertion (A): The sage could never bring his pigtail to the front.
Reason (R): A pigtail is fixed to the back of the head, and moving one's body will make the head and the pigtail move together. || கவிதையின் சூழலில் sage (முனிவர்) என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A || Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A || Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false || A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true || A is false but R is true
questions.ex: This assesses real-life logic (HOTS). The pigtail cannot move to the front of the face by simply spinning because it's attached to the back of the very head that is turning. || This assesses real-life logic (HOTS). The pigtail cannot move to the front of the face by simply spinning because it's attached to the back of the very head that is turning.

questions.q: Select the option that correctly groups words related to 'thinking/wondering' from the text. || Alas என்ற சொல் எந்த உணர்ச்சியைக் குறிக்கிறது?
options: sage, stout, slack || sage, stout, slack
mused, wondered, pondered || mused, wondered, pondered
twist, twirl, tack || twist, twirl, tack
yore, mystery, pigtail || yore, mystery, pigtail
questions.ex: Mused, wondered, and pondered all relate to the cognitive process of thinking deeply about the mystery. || Mused, wondered, and pondered all relate to the cognitive process of thinking deeply about the mystery.

questions.q: Which historical period in English Literature is Thackeray associated with? || ஆங்கில இலக்கியத்தில் தாக்கரே எந்த வரலாற்றுக் காலத்துடன் தொடர்புடையவர்?
options: The Renaissance || The Renaissance
The Middle Ages || The Middle Ages
The Victorian Age || The Victorian Age
The Modernist Period || The Modernist Period
questions.ex: He is mentioned as one of the great novelists of the English Victorian Age. || He is mentioned as one of the great novelists of the English Victorian Age.

questions.q: Complete the situation: If a person tries to change a universal truth or a physical constant using silly tricks, his actions can be compared to ________. || சூழ்நிலையை நிறைவு செய்யவும்: ஒரு நபர் உலகளாவிய உண்மையை மாற்ற முயன்றால், அவரது செயல்களை ________ உடன் ஒப்பிடலாம்.
options: Thackeray writing Vanity Fair || Thackeray writing Vanity Fair
The sage spinning to move his pigtail || The sage spinning to move his pigtail
A stout man walking || A stout man walking
A curious student asking questions || A curious student asking questions
questions.ex: This is a situation-based question. Trying to change a physical constant is an impossible task, just like the sage trying to make a back-attached pigtail hang in front by just spinning. || This is a situation-based question. Trying to change a physical constant is an impossible task, just like the sage trying to make a back-attached pigtail hang in front by just spinning.

questions.q: What does the line 'Because it hung behind him' indicate about the sage's mindset? || கவிதையின் சூழலில் sage (முனிவர்) என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: He was highly satisfied with his appearance || He was highly satisfied with his appearance
He was obsessed and unhappy with an unchangeable reality || He was obsessed and unhappy with an unchangeable reality
He was proud of his handsome pigtail || He was proud of his handsome pigtail
He wanted to hide his pigtail from others || He wanted to hide his pigtail from others
questions.ex: The line shows his sorrow and obsession over a natural anatomical fact that he couldn't accept. || The line shows his sorrow and obsession over a natural anatomical fact that he couldn't accept.

questions.q:
Match the following phrases with the correct grammatical/poetic function:
1. Curious case - i. Interjection expressing grief
2. Slack, tack, back - ii. Definition of Irony
3. Appearance vs Reality - iii. Alliteration
4. Alas! - iv. Rhyming words || முனிவர் யோசித்துக்கொண்டிருந்த வழக்கை விவரிக்க எந்த வார்த்தை பயன்படுத்தப்படுகிறது?
options: 1-iii, 2-iv, 3-ii, 4-i || 1-iii, 2-iv, 3-ii, 4-i
1-iv, 2-iii, 3-i, 4-ii || 1-iv, 2-iii, 3-i, 4-ii
1-ii, 2-i, 3-iv, 4-iii || 1-ii, 2-i, 3-iv, 4-iii
1-i, 2-ii, 3-iii, 4-iv || 1-i, 2-ii, 3-iii, 4-iv
questions.ex: Curious case is Alliteration. Slack, tack, back are rhyming words. Appearance vs Reality is Irony. Alas! is an interjection expressing grief. || Curious case is Alliteration. Slack, tack, back are rhyming words. Appearance vs Reality is Irony. Alas! is an interjection expressing grief.

questions.q: Ultimately, the message hidden inside this comic poem for the readers is: || கவிதையின் சூழலில் sage (முனிவர்) என்பதன் அர்த்தம் என்ன?
options: Always keep a pigtail at the back || Always keep a pigtail at the back
Do not waste energy on trying to change things that are naturally impossible to change || Do not waste energy on trying to change things that are naturally impossible to change
Spinning all day is good exercise || Spinning all day is good exercise
Sages are the smartest people on earth without any flaws || Sages are the smartest people on earth without any flaws
questions.ex: This Higher Order Thinking Skill (HOTS) question derives the moral. The sage's vain efforts highlight the foolishness of fighting natural, unchangeable facts. || This Higher Order Thinking Skill (HOTS) question derives the moral. The sage's vain efforts highlight the foolishness of fighting natural, unchangeable facts.
